chunk_summary: Guidelines for managing newborn weight loss include structured feeding reviews, supplementation steps, and stopping criteria to ensure hydration, weight stabilization, and breastfeeding success.
learning_objectives: Understand the clinical thresholds for newborn weight loss requiring intervention.
Learn the stepwise supplementation hierarchy for newborns with feeding difficulties.
Identify criteria for stopping supplementation and ensuring effective breastfeeding.
Recognize signs of maternal or infant-related issues requiring escalation of care.
key_facts: Trigger urgent feeding review when weight loss approaches 10% of birth weight.
Supplementation hierarchy: mother's expressed milk, donor human milk, then formula.
Use cup/spoon or paladai to protect breastfeeding skills during supplementation.
Reassess within 24–48 hours to monitor hydration, feeding calmness, and weight trends.
Stopping criteria: effective milk transfer, weight gain trend, and caregiver confidence.
reference_quote: Trigger urgent feeding review when weight loss approaches 10% of birth weight — Stanford Medicine.
document_key_topics: Newborn weight loss thresholds and intervention
